calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-24-2023 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-28-2022 Set For Case Management Conference On Apr-19-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Plaintiff Duncans Claims And 2 Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Apr-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jorge Hechavarria, Esq. (310.602.6050; jorge@dereksmithlaw.com) of Derek Smith Law Group, LLP for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

The court noted it will not hear oral argument since there was no compliance with tentative ruling rule.
Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT TRANSCEND INC.'S Petition To Compel Arbitration Of Plaintiff Duncans Claims And Motion To Stay:

Defendant Transcend Inc.'s petition to compel arbitration is granted.

"[T]he party moving to compel arbitration bears the burden of establishing the existence of a valid agreement to arbitrate." (Hotels Nevada v. L.A. Pac. Ctr., Inc. (2006) 144 Cal.App. 4th 754, 758; see also Rosenthal v. Great Western Fin. Securities Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 413.) Defendant submits sufficient evidence showing the offer letter contained a valid arbitration clause, which Plaintiff voluntarily signed February 25, 2020. (Brook Decl., 5 & Ex. 1 ("Offer Letter").) The arbitration clause states in pertinent part, "[a]ny controversy or claim arising out of this letter agreement and any and all claims relating to your employment with the Company will be settled by final and binding arbitration." (Offer Letter, 11.) "[T]he party opposing arbitration bears the burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence any fact necessary to [the] defense [of a valid agreement to arbitrate]." (Hotels Nevada v. L.A. Pac. Ctr., Inc. (2006) 144 Cal.App.4th 754, 758; Rosenthal v. Great Western Fin. Securities Corp. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 394, 413.) "[B]oth procedural and substantive unconscionability must be shown for the defense to be established." (OTO, LLC v. Ken Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 125.) Plaintiff has not done so. In this case, Plaintiff does not argue that the arbitration clause was procedurally or substantively invalid, rather Plaintiff argues that the primary purpose of the offer letter was to create an employment agreement making the clause unenforceable. Moreover, Plaintiff argues the language used in Provision 10 and 11 creates an irreconcilable conflict as to whether Plaintiff's claims must be arbitrated. Plaintiff further contends that when two clauses are in direct conflict with each other, the first clause must be received and the second clause must be rejected, therefore Provision 10 must govern. (See Hobson v. Mutual Ben. Health & Acc. Ass'n, (1950) 99 Cal. App. 2d 330.)

The Court finds Plaintiff's argument that the arbitration clause is invalid because it is contained in an offer letter and that Provision 10 supersedes Provision 11 lacks merit. First, California courts have routinely upheld arbitration provisions in offer letters. (See Farrar v. Direct Commerce, Inc., 9 Cal.App.5th 1257, 1268-69 (2017) (enforcing arbitration provision contained in offer letter); Peng v. First Republic Bank, 219 Cal. App. 4th 1462, 1466 (2013) (compelling arbitration based on clause in written employment offer); Jenks v. DLA Piper Rudnick Gray Cary US LLP, 243 Cal. App. 4th 1, 5 (2015) (same); Saheli v. White Memorial Medical Center, 21 Cal. App. 5th 308, 314-15 (2018) (enforcing arbitration provision in employment/training agreement and employee handbook); Roman v. Superior Court, 172 Cal. App. 4th 1462, 1470-71 (2009) (enforcing arbitration agreement in an employment application).) Second, as Defendant points out, there are many circumstances in which the arbitration clause at issue does not apply and the parties may resort to litigation rather than arbitration, for example "claims for sexual assault and other sexual harassment." (Offer Letter, 11.) Based on the foregoing, Defendant's motion to compel arbitration and stay is granted.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
